name: Aam Panna
tagline: Raw mango cooler with roasted cumin and mint — heat-stroke remedy

hero.alt: Pale green Aam Panna in a cut-crystal glass with ice, mint, roasted cumin, and a matching glass jug
hero.caption: aam panna — crystal glass, mint, cumin

steps.title: Boil the raw mango
steps.detail: Pressure cook the whole raw mangoes with 1 cup water for 3 whistles. Cool, peel, and squeeze the pulp off the seed. (Or roast the mangoes directly on a flame until soft inside, ~10 min.)
steps.tip: Roasting gives a smokier, deeper aam panna; boiling keeps it bright.

steps.title: Blend with seasonings
steps.detail: Blend the pulp with jaggery, roasted cumin, black salt, black pepper, mint leaves, and 1 cup water until smooth. This is your concentrate — store in the fridge.
steps.tip: Concentrate keeps for 2 weeks refrigerated — make once, drink for days.

steps.title: Dilute and serve
steps.detail: Mix 3 tbsp concentrate with 1 cup chilled water per glass. Top with ice and a sprig of mint. Stir and serve.
steps.tip: Stronger or weaker? Adjust the concentrate ratio — that's the joy of homemade panna.

healthFacts: Raw mango is exceptionally high in vitamin C — supports skin and iron absorption.
Natural electrolyte blend: potassium from mango, sodium from black salt.
Used traditionally to prevent heat-stroke and rehydrate after long sun exposure.
Low glycemic when made with minimal added sugar — choose jaggery over white sugar.
